macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Oil Surges 3% as Middle East Tensions Escalate; Strait of Hormuz Closed by Iran
items.summary: Oil prices jumped 3% after Iran claimed to close the vital Strait of Hormuz and fighting intensified in the Gulf, sending Brent crude to a two-week high and rattling energy and travel stocks.

items.title: Nasdaq Drops 3% in Early Trading Amid Tech Sell-Off Linked to Middle East Fears
items.summary: A sell-off in big technology companies spread from Asia to the U.S., pulling the Nasdaq Composite down 3% as investors weighed Middle East tensions and inflation risks from rising energy costs.

items.title: Fed Report: U.S. Inflation 'Stepped Up Further' Due to Tariffs, Energy Costs, and AI Buildout
items.summary: The Federal Reserve stated in a monetary policy report that U.S. inflation increased this spring, driven by evolving tariff impacts, war-related energy cost rises, and the booming artificial intelligence infrastructure buildout.

items.title: IMF Cuts 2026 Global Growth Forecast to 3% Citing Iran War Fallout
items.summary: The International Monetary Fund downgraded its 2026 world growth forecast to 3%, noting that while AI demand partly offsets the energy shock from the Iran war, the conflict has significantly dampened global economic outlook.

items.title: SK Hynix Raises $26.5B in Record-Breaking U.S. IPO, Eclipsing Alibaba's 2014 Debut
items.summary: South Korean chip giant SK Hynix completed the largest-ever IPO by a foreign firm in the U.S., raising $26.5 billion and warning of a historic memory chip shortage by 2027 due to soaring AI demand.

items.title: Bank of Canada Expected to Hold Rate at 2.25% Through 2027 as Price Pressures Contained
items.summary: A Reuters poll indicates the Bank of Canada will keep its overnight rate at 2.25% on July 15 and maintain it well into next year, as price pressures remain largely contained and the economy gradually recovers.

items.title: European Shares End Four-Week Winning Streak as Tech Unwind and Middle East Tensions Hit Markets
items.summary: European stocks fell on Friday, halting a four-week rally, as an unwind in tech stocks and resurgent Middle East tensions that rattled oil markets dampened investor sentiment.

items.title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh to Testify Tuesday Ahead of June CPI Data; SEC Tightens Activist Investor Rules
items.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh is scheduled to testify Tuesday morning just after June Consumer Price Index data is released, while the SEC simultaneously tightened disclosure rules for activist investors to raise transparency.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs Snap Eight-Week Outflow Streak with $197M–$282M Inflows as BTC Holds Near $62,800
items.summary: Bitcoin ETFs posted combined inflows of $197.4 million to $282 million, reversing an eight-week outflow trend, while Bitcoin traded near $62,806 (down ~2.1% in 24 hours) and total crypto market cap rose 1.72% to $2.16 trillion[1][2].

items.title: Michael Saylor Posts Cryptic Chart After $216M Bitcoin Sale: 'Orange Dots Tell Only Part of the Story'
items.summary: Strategy co-founder Michael Saylor shared a cryptic chart following a $216 million Bitcoin sale, hinting that the transaction reflects a broader strategic move beyond simple liquidation[1].

items.title: Hedera Lending Protocol Bonzo Lend Hit for $9M After Price Manipulation via Supra Verifier
items.summary: Bonzo Lend, a lending protocol on Hedera, was exploited for $9 million after a Supra verifier accepted a manipulated price update, marking a significant DeFi security breach[1].

items.title: U.S. CBDC Ban Passed into Law Without Trump’s Signature as Housing Bill Becomes Statute
items.summary: A housing bill including a four-year ban on U.S. central bank digital currencies (CBDC) passed into law without President Trump’s signature, reducing regulatory uncertainty for stablecoins through 2030[1][5].

items.title: DOJ to Drop Charges Against Man Accused in $722M BitClub Crypto Ponzi Scheme
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice plans to drop charges against an individual accused in the $722 million BitClub crypto Ponzi scheme, according to Bloomberg[1].

items.title: Circle Surges After OCC Grants Approval to Operate as Trust Bank
items.summary: Stablecoin issuer Circle saw a premarket surge after the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency approved it to operate as a trust bank, a major regulatory milestone[8].

items.title: SK Hynix’s $26.5B U.S. Listing Launched on Telegram via xStocks Platform
items.summary: SK Hynix’s $26.5 billion U.S. stock listing became accessible to Telegram users through xStocks, expanding retail access to traditional equity via blockchain infrastructure[1][3].

items.title: Kraken to Relaunch Mobile App with Agentic Trading and AI Advice at Core
items.summary: Kraken announced a mobile app relaunch centered on agentic trading and AI-driven advice, signaling a shift toward autonomous, AI-integrated crypto trading[1].

items.title: Iranian Entities Executed $3.84B in CoinEx Transactions to Circumvent U.S. Sanctions
items.summary: Blockchain analysis reveals Iranian entities conducted over $3.84 billion in transactions via CoinEx to evade U.S. economic sanctions, highlighting ongoing illicit crypto flows[3].

items.title: Total Crypto Liquidations Hit $988M in 24 Hours as BTC Drops Below $63K
items.summary: Crypto market liquidations totaled $988 million over the past 24 hours, with Bitcoin falling below $63,000 to trade at $62,997 (down 1.66% daily)[3][6].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Family and ChatGPT Work Agent
items.summary: OpenAI unveiled GPT-5.6, a new family of models, alongside ChatGPT Work, an agent designed to execute tasks across applications and files for workplace automation.

items.title: SpaceXAI Releases Grok 4.5, Described as 'Opus-Class' Model
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceXAI launched Grok 4.5, an Opus-class AI model promising higher efficiency and lower coding-task costs compared to rivals.

items.title: Meta Enters AI Coding Battle with Muse Spark 1.1 and Launches Data Center in Canada
items.summary: Meta released Muse Spark 1.1, a low-cost enterprise AI coding model, and announced its first Canadian data center in Alberta to support AI computing capacity.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Trade Secret Misappropriation for Consumer Hardware
items.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and two former employees alleging the misappropriation of trade secrets to benefit OpenAI's consumer hardware initiatives.

items.title: China Reports Security Vulnerabilities in Anthropic's Claude Code
items.summary: China identified security flaws in multiple versions of Anthropic's Claude Code that allow sensitive user data, including location and identity, to be sent to remote servers without consent.

items.title: South Korean Giants Samsung and SK Hynix Plan $518B Chipmaking Hub for AI
items.summary: Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix announced a combined $518 billion investment in a new chip manufacturing hub in South Korea to address soaring AI-driven demand.

items.title: Tata Consultancy Services Builds AI Engineering Team and Seeks Acquisitions
items.summary: TCS is forming a team of up to 8,900 forward-deployed engineers and pursuing AI acquisitions, betting that AI will create new business rather than undermine outsourcing.

items.title: Meta's AI Detection Tool Fails to Identify Cropped AI-Generated Images
items.summary: A Reuters analysis found that Meta's new AI detection tool, previewed alongside Muse Image, failed to identify its own AI-generated images once they were cropped.

items.title: OpenAI Limits GPT-5.6 Sol Release to Trump-Approved Customers Amid Cybersecurity Review
items.summary: OpenAI restricted the rollout of its new GPT-5.6 Sol model to customers approved by President Trump's administration during an ongoing cybersecurity review.

items.title: Industry Shift Toward Cost-Efficient AI Systems for Enterprise Use
items.summary: The AI sector is pivoting from leaderboard rankings to selecting models based on task, cost, and control, with new releases like GPT-5.6 and Grok 4.5 emphasizing efficiency.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Sony Announces PlayStation Games Will Be Digital-Only Starting January 2028
items.summary: Sony confirmed that new PlayStation games will no longer be released on physical discs starting January 2028, marking a full transition to digital distribution via the PlayStation Store or download codes.

items.title: Obsidian Entertainment and Bethesda Game Studios Collaborate on New Fallout Game After Layoffs
items.summary: Following recent mass layoffs, Obsidian Entertainment is working with Bethesda Game Studios on a new Fallout game, as reported by Bloomberg.

items.title: Nintendo Ends Support for Mario Kart Tour on September 30
items.summary: Nintendo will shut down support for the mobile game Mario Kart Tour on September 30 at 8:00 AM, ending the service after years of operation.

items.title: Hwaeyang Life e-Sports Wins First MSI Title as LCK's Top Seed
items.summary: Hwaeyang Life e-Sports, the LCK's number 1 seed, secured their inaugural MSI championship, marking a historic win for the team.

items.title: Arknights: Endfield Launches Half-Anniversary Update 'Into the Abyss' on July 16
items.summary: The mobile game Arknights: Endfield will release its major half-anniversary update titled 'Into the Abyss' on July 16, 2026.

items.title: Sudden Attack: Zero Point Enters Early Access on Steam July 30
items.summary: Nexon's FPS game Sudden Attack: Zero Point will begin early access on Steam on July 30, 2026, with a free-to-play model and a published 2026 roadmap.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Tops Steam Revenue List in 2026
items.summary: Reports indicate that Forza Horizon 6 has become the top Steam release by gross revenue in 2026, surpassing other major titles.

items.title: Game 'Washing the Carpet' Released on July 13
items.summary: A unique game titled 'Washing the Carpet' (絨毯を洗うゲーム) officially launched on July 13, 2026.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Stolen Trade Secrets for AI Gadgets
items.summary: Apple filed a bombshell lawsuit against OpenAI and two former employees, alleging misappropriation of trade secrets to develop the AI company's upcoming consumer hardware, escalating tensions between the two tech giants.

items.title: Apple Stock Surges 15% as Investors Flee AI Selloff
items.summary: Apple's shares returned to record territory, adding nearly $600 billion in market value since June 25, as investors pivot away from chipmakers and cloud giants amid nervousness about AI spending.

items.title: EU Charges Meta's Instagram and Facebook with Breaching Tech Rules
items.summary: European regulators formally charged Meta with violating tech rules by designing addictive features like autoplay and infinite scroll, demanding changes to avoid steep fines.

items.title: Meta to Invest $9.1 Billion in First AI Data Center in Canada
items.summary: Meta announced it will invest over 13 billion Canadian dollars ($9.1 billion) to build its first artificial intelligence data center in Canada, marking its largest data center project in the country.

items.title: Samsung Announces Next-Gen UFS 5.0 Storage for Faster On-Device Performance
items.summary: Samsung Electronics unveiled its next-generation Universal Flash Storage 5.0 solution, designed to deliver significantly faster performance for future smartphones, wearables, and XR devices.

items.title: Samsung Launches Galaxy A27 5G in India with 120Hz Display
items.summary: Samsung officially launched the Galaxy A27 5G in India at a price of ₹28,999, featuring a 120Hz display and integrated AI capabilities.

items.title: Spain's Submer Group Invests $2 Billion in Madhya Pradesh Semiconductor Sector
items.summary: Submer Group announced a $2 billion investment in India's Madhya Pradesh semiconductor sector, expected to create approximately 5,000 direct employment opportunities.

items.title: Equiti Opens New Bangalore Technology Hub for Global Growth
items.summary: Dubai-based Equiti launched a new technology hub in Bangalore to tap India's engineering talent for supporting client platforms, cybersecurity, and global growth initiatives.
